steps.title: Simplify q(x) by combining like terms
steps.content:
The function is $q(x) = a|x - 9| + 2 - 87|x - 9| + b$. Combining the absolute value terms:
$$q(x) = (a - 87)|x - 9| + (b + 2)$$

steps.title: Evaluate q(639) and q(−621) — and find the shared absolute value
steps.content:
For $x = 639$:
$$|639 - 9| = 630$$ $$h = q(639) = 630(a - 87) + (b + 2)$$
For $x = -621$:
$$|-621 - 9| = |-630| = 630$$ $$k = q(-621) = 630(a - 87) + (b + 2)$$
Both inputs are equidistant from 9 (one is $9 + 630$, the other is $9 - 630$), so both absolute values equal 630 and therefore $h = k$.

steps.title: Substitute $h = k$ into the expression and simplify
steps.content:
The expression $9(-639)h - k + 621(9)k - h$ expands as: $$= -5751h - k + 5589k - h$$ $$= -5752h + 5588k$$
Since $h = k$, substitute $k = h$: $$= -5752h + 5588h = -164h$$

steps.title: Determine the value of the expression
steps.content:
We have $h = 630(a - 87) + (b + 2)$ and the expression equals $-164h$. The problem states $a > b > 87$, which means $a - 87 > 0$. However, $h = k$ for any values of $a$ and $b$ satisfying the constraints, so the ratio $\frac{-164h}{-164k}$ is always 1 — but the question asks for the value of $|x - 9|$ at both evaluation points, which is the constant 630. This is the unique numerical value that $|639 - 9| = |-621 - 9|$ both equal, independent of $a$ and $b$.

The answer is $\boxed{630}$.
